medical_history.chief_complaint: Multiple anomalies and dysmorphic features, including cleft palate, flat midface, hypertelorism, creases under the eyes, a small nose with anteverted nares, arachnodactyly of fingers and toes, laterally deviated great toes, and mild pectus carinatum
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Born via Cesarean section at 35 weeks 5 days gestational age. Pregnancy was complicated by polyhydramnios starting at 21 weeks GA. At 22 weeks 3 days GA, an intracardiac echogenic focus and nuchal thickening were noted. At 23 weeks 1 day GA, left clubfoot was noted. At birth, APGAR scores were 8 and 8 at 1 and 5 min, respectively, but around 5 min, she developed respiratory distress requiring CPAP and admission to the NICU. She remained in the NICU for 6 weeks due to respiratory issues and slowing feeding.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Normal karyotype (46, XX). Head ultrasound performed 2 days after birth showed mild prominence of the lateral ventricles and no evidence of hemorrhage. Brain MRI performed at 5 weeks of age was overall normal. At 9 weeks of age, she was evaluated for bilateral wrist contractures.
medical_history.family_history: Significant for a brother and father with similar physical features. Her 17-month-old brother previously presented at 1 day of life with a long neck, excess nuchal skin, large hands and feet with long fingers and toes, and pectus excavatum. He also had a flat midface and a saddle nose. The father had similar features including long fingers and toes, pectus excavatum, and laterally deviated great toes. He also had anteverted nares and mild hypertelorism.

physical_examination.general_examination: Respiratory distress requiring CPAP and admission to the NICU. Multiple anomalies and dysmorphic features, including cleft palate, flat midface, hypertelorism, creases under the eyes, a small nose with anteverted nares, arachnodactyly of fingers and toes, laterally deviated great toes, and mild pectus carinatum. Failed newborn hearing examination in both ears.
